title: Agenomic Workflow (v0.2)
description: Declarative description of a workflow: an ordered, possibly branching and long-running composition of agent calls, deterministic tool steps, human gates, signal waits, loops, and sub-workflows (RFC 0009). The manifest describes design, not execution state.

spec_version.description: Pins the Agenomic specification version this workflow targets.

id.description: Stable workflow identifier of the form workflow://<org>/<name>.

domain.description: Free-form domain tag (e.g. claims, support, onboarding).

version.description: Version of the workflow definition itself (semver recommended).

engine.description: Optional, non-normative hint about the runtime executing this workflow (e.g. temporal, langgraph, crewai, custom). Conformant tooling MUST NOT require it.

triggers.description: Optional declaration of how this workflow starts.

state.description: Reference to the shared context schema steps read from and write to. Only the schema is referenced; runtime state is NEVER bundled (RFC 0006 applies).

schema_path.description: Bundle-relative path to the state schema artifact.

steps.description: The workflow's steps. Execution order is the DAG induced by `depends_on`; steps with identical dependencies MAY run in parallel. Step `when` guards make any step conditional.

signals.description: External signals this workflow can receive while running (e.g. documents_received, expert_report_received).

escalation_rules.description: Declarative escalation table: when `condition` holds, the run is routed to `route` (a human role, team, or queue).

kind.description: Runtime family, e.g. temporal, langgraph, crewai, autogen, custom.

event.description: `event` triggers only: the event name that starts a run.

signal.description: `signal` triggers only: the signal name that starts a run.

payload_schema_path.description: Optional bundle-relative path to the signal payload schema.

condition.description: Free-form condition tag or expression (e.g. bodily_injury, suspected_fraud).

route.description: Human role, team, or queue that receives the escalation.

duration.description: Plain duration literal: integer + unit, e.g. 500ms, 30s, 15m, 2h, 90d.

role.description: Human role required to act, e.g. senior_handler, risk_officer.

escalation_route.description: Role, team, or queue receiving the case when the SLA elapses.

mode.description: Resume on the first declared signal (any) or after all of them (all). Defaults to any.

id.description: Step identifier, unique within the workflow.

type.description: agent: invoke an Agenomic agent; tool: deterministic tool/function call; human: human gate; wait: suspend until a signal; workflow: invoke a sub-workflow; loop: repeat a body of steps until a condition holds.

agent.description: `agent` steps only: the agent invoked by this step.

skill.description: `agent` steps only: optional skill id within the agent.

tool.description: `tool` steps only: the deterministic tool or function invoked.

gate.description: `human` steps only: who must act, and what happens on SLA breach.

wait_for.description: `wait` steps only: which signals resume the run.

uses.description: `workflow` steps only: path or URI of the sub-workflow manifest.

body.description: `loop` steps only: the steps repeated on each iteration.

until.description: `loop` steps only: condition that terminates the loop.

max_iterations.description: `loop` steps only: hard upper bound on iterations.

depends_on.description: Step ids that must complete before this step starts. Steps with identical dependencies MAY run in parallel.

when.description: Optional guard expression over the shared state; when false the step is skipped (enables conditional routing and early exit).

on_error.description: Behavior after retries are exhausted. Defaults to fail.
